tools_page.heading: Tools
tools_page.description: Maintenance actions for monitor scheduling and health.

deleted_slug_check.title: Deleted Slug Check
deleted_slug_check.description: Checks if a platform slug is blocked for reuse (30-day protection) and when it unblocks.

slug_cache_rebuild.title: Slug Cache Rebuild
slug_cache_rebuild.description: Rebuilds cache/status_page_slugs_cache.json from status_pages and updates in-memory slug cache for TLS allow checks.

swal.confirm_text: This will regenerate slug cache from status_pages table.

suppressed_results.title: Suppressed Monitor Results
suppressed_results.description: Inspect recently suppressed monitor DOWN samples (self-hosted guard/deploy guard) and reason counts.

auto_heal.title: Auto-Heal Next Check
auto_heal.description: Fixes missing/invalid next_check_at on enabled tool monitors by setting it to the next UTC-aligned boundary (daily at 00:00 UTC, or 6-hourly at 00/06/12/18 UTC).
auto_heal.help: Only updates rows where scheduling is missing/invalid; keeps aligned schedules intact.

realign.title: Realign Next Check (UTC)
realign.description: Forces next_check_at on enabled tool monitors to the next UTC-aligned boundary even if it is currently in the future but off-schedule.

swal.confirm_text: This will overwrite next_check_at for enabled tool monitors of the selected type.

remove_confirm.body: This will disable analytics for the selected service.

jsonl.text: Monitors, incidents, and monitor results use JSON Lines format (one JSON object per line) for efficient processing of large datasets. This allows streaming and processing of huge lists without loading entire files into memory.

create_flow.generic_error: Something went wrong while creating the monitor.
create_flow.include_failed: Failed to include monitor on status page.
create_flow.include_prompt_text: You can always change this later in Status Page Settings.

create_flow.include_success_text: The monitor is now visible on your status page.

edit.heading: Edit Monitor
edit.delete_warning: This will permanently delete this monitor and all its historical data. This action cannot be undone.

form.advanced_settings_hint: Advanced is selected because this monitor already uses settings hidden in Simple mode.
form.api_mode_help: Simple shows only the URL, method, auth, and an optional response-body text match. Advanced shows request setup, response assertions, interval, impact, and other monitor settings.
form.component_help: Select which component this monitor belongs to on your status page.
form.default_success_hint: No custom response check is set. The monitor is UP on 2xx/3xx responses and DOWN on everything else.
form.expected_result_help: Optional. Leave this empty to treat 2xx/3xx responses as UP. If you fill it and do not add advanced response assertions, the monitor is UP only when the response is 2xx/3xx and the response body contains this text.
form.request_headers_help: Sent with the request. Not validated unless you add response assertions.
form.request_body_help: Sent as the raw request body for POST, PUT, and PATCH requests. Set Content-Type header accordingly (e.g. application/json, application/xml).
form.request_query_params_help: Appended to the request URL. Not validated unless you add response assertions.
form.request_section_help: These settings control what the monitor sends. They do not determine UP/DOWN on their own.
form.response_assertions_help: Optional. If you add rules here, they override the simple response text match and the default 2xx/3xx check.
form.response_section_help: These rules determine whether the monitor is UP or DOWN.

list.intro: Create runbooks to guide your team through incident response
list.empty_description:
Create your first runbook to document incident response procedures.
Runbooks are automatically linked to incidents based on the affected component.
list.how_it_works_description: Assign a runbook to a specific component or to an entire status page. When a new incident is created for that component, the runbook is automatically linked and shown in the incident sidebar.

delete.title: Delete runbook?
delete.text: This will unlink the runbook from all incidents. This action cannot be undone.

hero.title: A status page without the complexity
hero.subtitle: No monitors to configure. No integrations to wire up. Create a simple status page, update component statuses from a single dashboard, and keep your users informed with context-rich notes that automatically create incident history.

benefits.title: Everything you need, nothing you don't
benefits.subtitle: Tell your users what's up - without setting up a single monitor.

launch.title: Up and running in minutes
launch.description: Create a status page, add your services as components, and share the link. No monitors, no agents, no infrastructure required.

upgrade.title: Upgrade when you're ready
upgrade.description: Start simple and add automated uptime monitoring, custom domains, branding and incident management as your product grows.

dashboard.title: One-screen control panel
dashboard.description: The Simple Dashboard puts everything in one place — component statuses, active incidents, and quick updates. No navigating around, no settings pages.

notes.title: Add context with notes
notes.description: When changing a component's status, add an optional note (e.g. "Investigating elevated error rates"). It shows publicly next to the component — and automatically creates an incident record.

notifications.title: Subscribers notified automatically
notifications.description: When you add a note to a status change, your page's subscribers are notified automatically — no separate incident to create, no extra steps.

history.title: 90-day reported history
history.description: Every component shows a 90-day strip of reported status — green for operational days, coloured ticks for incidents, overrides, or maintenance. A transparent record for your users.

use_cases.title: Who uses simple status pages?
use_cases.subtitle: Any team that wants to communicate service health without automation.

startups.title: Startups in early access
startups.description: You have a handful of early users and want to show them you take reliability seriously, but you're not ready to invest in full monitoring infrastructure.
startups.point_1: Add components for your API, dashboard, and auth service
startups.point_2: Update status with a note from the dashboard during incidents
startups.point_3: Share the URL in your app footer

internal.title: Internal tools & admin panels
internal.description: Your internal systems don't face public users, but your teammates still need to know when things are broken or in maintenance.
internal.point_2: Set maintenance status before scheduled downtime
internal.point_3: Share with your team on Slack

indie.title: Indie makers & side projects
indie.description: You run a solo project and want a professional status page without paying for monitoring you don't need yet.
indie.point_1: Free plan covers up to 3 components
indie.point_3: Looks great and builds user trust

how_it_works.title: Three steps to a live status page
how_it_works.subtitle: No guides needed. No technical setup. Just fill in the blanks.

step_1.title: Create your status page
step_1.description: Choose "Simple Status Page" when creating a new page. Give it a name - it's ready instantly.

step_2.title: Add your components
step_2.description: Add the services, APIs or features you want to track - for example "API", "Dashboard", "Auth". Each becomes a row on your public page.

step_3.title: Update from your dashboard
step_3.description: When something changes, open the Simple Dashboard, pick a status and add an optional note. Your public page updates instantly — and subscribers are notified if you included a note.

statuses.title: Five status states, clearly communicated
statuses.subtitle: Each component on your page can be set to one of five states that visitors instantly understand - no jargon, no ambiguity.

degraded.label: Degraded
degraded.description: Working but slower or partially broken

major_outage.label: Major Outage
major_outage.description: Service is down for most users

cta.title: Ready to keep your users in the loop?
cta.subtitle: Create a free simple status page in under two minutes. No credit card required.

dashboard_feature.title: Update status in two clicks
dashboard_feature.description: The Simple Dashboard is your home base for the page. Every component is listed with its current status and note. Click edit, pick a state, optionally add a note, and save — done.
dashboard_feature.illustration_label: Simple Dashboard — everything in one screen
dashboard_feature.point_1: See all component statuses and active incidents at a glance
dashboard_feature.point_2: Pin it as your default landing page for instant access after login
dashboard_feature.point_3: No navigating around settings — everything is right there

notes_feature.title: One note does three things
notes_feature.description: When you add a note alongside a status change (e.g. "Investigating elevated error rates"), the platform automatically shows it next to the component on your public page, creates an incident record in your history, and notifies subscribers — all from a single form.
notes_feature.illustration_label: Notes shown publicly · Incident created automatically
notes_feature.point_1: Note appears inline on the public status page
notes_feature.point_2: Incident entry created automatically — no separate step
notes_feature.point_3: Subscribers notified without opening the incident management flow
notes_feature.point_4: Incident auto-resolves when you reset the component to Operational

simple_status_page.title: Simple Status Page
simple_status_page.description: No monitors needed - update status manually

embeds.heading: Status Page Embeds & Widgets
embeds.intro: Copy & paste these snippets into your website or docs. Replace YOUR_SLUG with your status page slug.

live_widget.description: Inline script that renders a themed card. Supports light/dark/auto themes, optional incidents list, refresh interval.
live_widget.heading: Live Widget (Script)

markdown.description: Use these in GitHub README, Zendesk, Notion, etc. Badge reflects live overall status.
markdown.heading: Markdown Snippets

preview.compact_badge_description: Compact badge: inline response time sparkline (last 45 min).
preview.compact_incidents_description: Compact + incidents list (if any), adaptive to user theme.
preview.description: Live examples (all use your first status page slug if available). Copy the matching code below each preview.
preview.full_card_description: Full card: incidents + both sparklines + auto refresh.
preview.full_widget_disabled_link_description: Full widget with link disabled (static card).
preview.heading: Preview

svg.description: Cached simple badge. No JavaScript. Good for README or docs.
svg.heading: SVG Badge

wordpress.description: Official WordPress plugin with Gutenberg block, classic widget, and shortcodes.
wordpress.heading: WordPress Plugin
